text: A pyramidal-to-polarized training intensity distribution sequence over 16 weeks produces the largest improvements in VO2peak, lactate thresholds, and 5-km performance compared to purely pyramidal, purely polarized, or polarized-to-pyramidal sequences in well-trained runners.
Switching from pyramidal to polarized training intensity distribution yields approximately 3.0% improvement in relative VO2peak over 16 weeks in well-trained runners.
Switching from pyramidal to polarized training intensity distribution yields approximately 1.5% improvement in 5-km time trial performance over 16 weeks in well-trained runners.
Switching from pyramidal to polarized distribution improves velocity at 2 mmol/L blood lactate by approximately 1.7% and velocity at 4 mmol/L blood lactate by approximately 1.5% over 16 weeks.

All four training periodization approaches (pyramidal, polarized, pyramidal-to-polarized, polarized-to-pyramidal) effectively improve endurance surrogates and performance in well-trained runners when training load is held constant.

When sequencing training intensity distributions, building a pyramidal base before transitioning to polarized training is superior to the reverse order (polarized then pyramidal) for maximizing performance gains.
Relative VO2peak is the primary physiological correlate of performance improvement when transitioning from pyramidal to polarized training intensity distribution.
